human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for aerospace and defense mission-support boundary discipline, source quality, traceability and inventory handling, quality hold and release escalation, supplier substitution refusal, service workflow coverage, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to mission-support service intake, readiness and blocker summaries, stakeholder communication, traceability-aware analysis, and escalation packaging.
Authority sources cover DoD product support, DoD materiel management, NASA life-cycle logistics, FAA production and release boundaries, IAQG aerospace QMS posture, and DCMA quality surveillance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal readiness resolution, missing prerequisites, policy exception refusal, traceability conflict, quality hold and release escalation, supplier substitution refusal, logistics handoff, repeat failure escalation, unsupported workarounds, terminology handling, source conflict escalation, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/aerospace-and-defense/mission-support-specialist/ so mission-support work is distinct from adjacent quality, planning, inventory, procurement, supplier quality, logistics, engineering, security, and contract lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around release overrides, quality holds, supplier substitutions, engineering dispositions, government acceptance, security exceptions, and policy overrides.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated aerospace and defense mission-support specialist for evidence-backed service resolution, stakeholder communication, traceability-aware analysis, and controlled escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
